examples: These are substances which specifically latch onto the protein on the cell membrane.
In pterosaurs a greatly elongated fourth finger of each forelimb supported a wing-like membrane.
Archaeopteryx shows no obvious features of gliders, such as a broad membrane connecting forelimbs and hind limbs.
These protuberances perch on a cell's membrane and serve as gateways to the outside.
Theravance's drug grips the bacterial membrane more tightly than vancomcyin, enabling it to kill faster.
